body:
{
  "title": "1:1 Seren Voss + Harper Nguyen — June 15, 2027",
  "compiled_truth": "Weekly 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (VP of Engineering) and [Harper Nguyen](people/harper-nguyen) (Senior Systems Engineer) held Tuesday afternoon, June 15, 2027. Harper was running about ten minutes late — had been deep in a profiling session on the ingest pipeline and lost track of time. Seren didn't mind; she'd been reviewing the Q3 staffing plan anyway.\n\nBulk of the conversation centered on the single-pass primitive refactor that Harper has been leading for the last six weeks. Harper reported that the deterministic authentication layer is now passing all regression tests on the new memory model, but flagged a subtle race condition that only manifests under sustained 40Gbps throughput. Seren asked whether this was related to the issue [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) hit during the Bellmark Labs stress test back in April; Harper confirmed it's the same family of bugs but a different trigger path. They agreed Harper would document the root cause analysis and loop in two junior engineers for pairing sessions this week to spread knowledge of the subsystem.\n\nSeren also brought up team dynamics. She'd noticed some tension between Harper and the DevOps pod around deployment cadence. Harper acknowledged it — said the friction is mostly about staging environment availability. The DevOps team wants longer soak periods, Harper's team wants faster iteration cycles. Seren suggested they propose a shared calendar for staging slots and bring it to the next cross-team sync on Thursday. Harper agreed this was reasonable.\n\nThey briefly touched on Harper's career trajectory. Seren mentioned that a Staff Engineer leveling conversation is on the horizon, probably Q4. She encouraged Harper to start building a promo packet now — focusing on the single-pass work, the mentorship of newer hires, and the cross-team collaboration improvements. Harper seemed genuinely surprised and appreciative.\n\nFinally, Seren flagged that Anchor's leadership team is considering a small internal hackathon in late July to explore new applications of the deterministic infrastructure stack. She wanted Harper to think about whether they'd want to lead a team or participate as an individual contributor. Harper said they'd think on it and get back by end of week.\n\nMeeting ran about 35 minutes total. No major action items beyond the race condition writeup and the staging calendar proposal.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2027-04-22** | Bellmark Labs stress test surfaces throughput-related race conditions in [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) ingest pipeline",
    "- **2027-05-03** | [Harper Nguyen](people/harper-nguyen) begins leading single-pass primitive refactor project",
    "- **2027-06-08** | Previous 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and Harper; initial discussion of DevOps pod friction",
    "- **2027-06-12** | Regression tests pass on new memory model for authentication layer",
    "- **2027-06-15** | This 1:1 meeting — race condition flagged, staging calendar proposed, Staff Engineer leveling discussed",
    "- **2027-06-17** | Cross-team sync scheduled to present shared staging calendar proposal",
    "- **2027-06-22** | Harper submits root cause analysis document for 40Gbps race condition",
    "- **2027-07-28** | Internal hackathon at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) tentatively planned",
    "- **2027-10-01** | Estimated timeline for [Harper Nguyen](people/harper-nguyen) Staff Engineer leveling conversation"
  ]
}
